perspective: Architecture parity: a faithful 1:1 mapping of next-supabase-agent-harness to the Tauri 2 + React 19/Vite + Hono + Drizzle/Postgres 16 stack. Every component of the original is classified carries-over-verbatim / re-pointed / dropped / new-for-stack, preserving the load-bearing properties: single-source-of-truth gate config, direct invocation (no package.json script indirection), three-tier hook exit-code contract, two-layer tamper evidence plus CI min-floor, and bootstrap-green selftest as the release acceptance test.
summary:
tauri-postgres-agent-harness is a structural clone of the sibling harness with three deliberate deltas forced by the stack: (1) the generated project is a pnpm monorepo (apps/desktop with src-tauri, apps/server, packages/schema|importer|eval|config), so all gates run from the repo root against workspace-wide tools (tsc -b over project references, eslint flat with projectService, knip workspaces map, depcruise over apps+packages, one root vitest.config.ts with v4 projects) — the single-config-at-root discipline is what keeps the write-guard PROTECTED list small and the gate config a single source of truth; (2) Rust/Tauri is a second toolchain that cannot be a hard default gate (cold clippy compiles for minutes; fresh installs may lack cargo), so every Rust/DB/cargo-dependent gate reuses the original's proven asymmetry — self-skip honestly when the prerequisite is absent locally, fail closed in CI — which is also how bootstrap-green survives the heavier stack; (3) the trust boundary moved from RSC/DAL-in-one-process to a client/server network boundary, so the Supabase invariants (getSession ban, service_role ban, proxy.ts) map cleanly onto new ones (never trust unverified Entra tokens — jose jwtVerify with iss/aud/tid pinning; the migrator DSN is the service_role analog, banned outside migration tooling; Tauri capabilities are not authorization — authz lives in the Hono DAL over FORCE RLS with SET LOCAL GUCs).

Everything else carries: hookio.mjs verbatim; write-guard/bash-guard rule tables re-pointed; stop-validate-gate verbatim; validate.mjs --min-floor mechanism verbatim with a new FLOOR; manifest-based installer with owned/seeded/config ownership verbatim, with detect.mjs rewritten for pnpm-workspace detection and merge-never-clobber extended per-workspace; dual-channel packaging (npx installer + Claude Code plugin) unchanged; the selftest matrix gains a windows-latest `tauri build --debug` leg and a deliberate-violation canary job. New default gates encode the highest-leverage research findings that are fast and false-positive-safe (tauri-policy JSON asserts, schema-RLS manifest, contract-drift on openapi.json + migrations, post-build bundle grep); everything heavy or environment-sensitive (WDIO .exe E2E, Windows signing pipeline, mutation, pgBackRest drills, perf budgets, macOS) ships as opt-in modules.

## VALIDATE_STEPS (tools/harness.config.mjs — cheap→expensive, break on first failure)

| # | step | command | catches | ~runtime | research encoded |
|---|------|---------|---------|----------|------------------|
| 1 | format | `pnpm exec biome ci .` | formatting + fast correctness/a11y lint, whole workspace | 2–5s | T2r4 (Biome sole formatter; single-owner rule matrix); generated files (drizzle/*.sql+snapshots, bindings.ts, openapi.json, dist-isolation/) excluded |
| 2 | types | `pnpm exec tsc -b` | max-strict flags (noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, verbatimModuleSyntax…) via packages/config base; project references = free architecture check; cross-package contract drift | 5–20s incremental | T2r0/r1/r2 (TS 6.0.x pinned via catalog; references topology; erasableSyntaxOnly on server) |
| 3 | lint | `pnpm exec eslint . --max-warnings 0` | strictTypeChecked + no-floating-promises family; boundary bans (desktop↛drizzle/pg, dal-only db import, jose-only-in-auth/, ipc-only raw invoke); parse-don't-validate bans (assertions, JSON.parse outside parsers, process.env outside env.ts); cognitive-complexity error (replaces blanket max-lines per T9r7, ratchet script for warn-count) | 20–45s | T2r3/r8, T6r0/r2/r6, T7r3/r5, T13r1/r9, T9r1/r7 |
| 4 | provenance | `node tools/check-sources.mjs` | decision sites lacking `// SOURCE:` (regex lockstep with PostToolUse hook) | ~1s | carried doctrine |
| 5 | contracts | `node tools/check-contract-drift.mjs` | (a) drizzle-kit generate produces no pending migration (schema↔SQL sync); (b) regenerated openapi.json ≠ committed (side-effect-free route-tree import); (c) tauri-specta bindings.ts drift — skip-with-warning if cargo absent, fail closed in CI | 3–8s | T11r7 (committed spec always truthful), T2r7, T1r3, T5 pitfall (drizzle rename=silent DROP shows up as reviewable SQL) |
| 6 | schema-rls | `node tools/check-rls-manifest.mjs` | walks exported Drizzle pgTables (tsx import, no DB): every table carries per-operation pgPolicy set or is listed in tools/rls-exempt.json with a reason | 2–4s | T5r0/r3 (policies as TS-visible schema objects; per-op not FOR ALL) |
| 7 | tauri-policy | `node tools/check-tauri-policy.mjs` | pure-JSON asserts on tauri.conf.json + capabilities/*.json + identity.lock.json (details in hooks_and_guards) | <1s | T0r0/r3/r7, T1r0/r1/r4/r5, T7r4, T13r3 |
| 8 | screens | `node tools/check-screen-manifest.mjs` | every SPA route registered in screen manifest (iterated by a11y/skew specs) and wrapped in an ErrorBoundary — the check-route-boundaries analog | <1s | T3r7 (route-manifest so new pages can't ship unscanned), T10r6 |
| 9 | aliveness | `node tools/check-aliveness-adoption.mjs` | manifest+union-scan: screens still import keyboard-registry / query-kit / connection-status primitives | <1s | carried pattern; T7r3/r7, T13r7 |
| 10 | dead-code | `pnpm exec knip` | unused exports/files/deps across the workspace map (knip 6 monorepo mode; `--fix` denied by bash-guard) | 8–20s | T2r5, T9r2 |
| 11 | architecture | `pnpm exec depcruise apps packages tools --config .dependency-cruiser.cjs` | desktop↛server/drizzle/pg; schema imports nothing from apps; only packages/eval/src/adapters touches inference endpoints; routes↛db (dal only); no-circular | 5–15s | T2r6, T6r0, T8r2 |
| 12 | build | `node tools/build-check.mjs` = vite build (desktop) + tsc emit (server) + **post-build grep of dist/ for forbidden markers** (drizzle-orm, node:pg, MIGRATOR_, VITE_*KEY literals) | 20–40s | T2r6 bundle backstop, T1r9 (Vite env inlining leak) |
| 13 | rust | `node tools/run-rust-gates.mjs` — `cargo fmt --check` + `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings`; runs only when src-tauri/** dirty or CI=true; **self-skips (printed SKIP) if cargo absent locally, exits 1 in CI** | skip / 10–30s warm | T1r7, T3r4, T10r4; reuses run-rls skip/fail-closed asymmetry |

Commented opt-in steps (uncomment via HARNESS_ALLOW_SELF_EDIT after enabling module): `styleguide`, `screen-registry`.

## STOP_HOOK_STEPS (direct invocation, never script names — verbatim doctrine)
1. `['validate', 'node tools/validate.mjs']`
2. `['rls-isolation', 'node tests/rls/run-rls.mjs']` — probes DATABASE_URL / local compose Postgres; when reachable: migrate scratch DB → catalog gate (relforcerowsecurity + polcmd coverage r/a/w/d + security_invoker views + matview-grant allowlist + app_api role-attribute asserts, all via node-postgres, no pgTAP dependency) → cross-tenant vitest project with seeded positive controls and 42501 asserts; locally unreachable = green SKIP; **CI + migrations present + unreachable = exit 1 "failing closed"** (verbatim doctrine, T5r5)
3. `['unit', 'pnpm exec vitest run --project unit-node --project unit-dom --silent']`

Warm full Stop gate ≈ 90–150s on the bootstrap app — comparable to the original's next-build-dominated chain, inside the 600s hook timeout. Heavy lanes (browser-mode vitest, WDIO .exe, mutation, cargo test/nextest, perf budgets) are CI/module-only per T3 pitfall ("every lane in the Stop gate makes agents game it").

## FLOOR (hardcoded in validate.mjs, run by CI `--min-floor`)
format, types, lint, provenance, contracts, schema-rls, tauri-policy, dead-code, architecture, build — i.e. the universally-passing steps; omits project-shaped screens/aliveness and toolchain-dependent rust (CI runs an unconditional rust job separately). Names must stay identical to VALIDATE_STEPS entries (merge is by name — verbatim gotcha).

## False-positive story per gate
format/lint/types: deterministic, config-scoped, eslint-disable-with-description escape; provenance: comment-skip + 3-line lookback + tests/generated excluded; contracts: regen-and-diff is exact, skip conditions printed; schema-rls: rls-exempt.json with reasons; tauri-policy: identity changes go through identity.lock.json edit in the same change (HARNESS_ALLOW_SELF_EDIT, human-in-loop); screens/aliveness: data manifests with exemption entries; dead-code: knip.json overrides for dynamic refs (Tauri command strings, GBNF assets) — protected file, human-reviewed; architecture: named rules with explanation strings; build: literal marker list; rust/rls: SKIP is printed with the reason, never silent, and only CI hard-fails.
hooks_and_guards:
## Hook set (three-tier contract via lib/hookio.mjs — carried verbatim: pass()=exit 0; denyTool()=exit 0+JSON permissionDecision:'deny' for PreToolUse; block()=exit 2+stderr for PostToolUse/Stop)

**pretool-bash-guard.mjs** (matcher Bash; [regex,message] table, tripwire-not-sandbox):
- carried verbatim: `rm -rf` permutations, `git push --force|-f|--force-with-lease`, `git reset --hard`, fork bomb, `.env` reads via cat/grep/sed/etc, `DROP TABLE|SCHEMA|DATABASE` in psql -c
- new: `/drizzle-kit\s+push\b/` → "push mutates live DBs with no history; use generate+migrate" (T5r4); `/knip\s+.*--fix/` (T2r5); `/\bcargo\s+update\b/` and `/\bpnpm\s+(up|update)\b/` → "lockfile changes go through Renovate" (T0r6); `/TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY|AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET|repo1-cipher-pass/` in any command (T0r4, T11r0); `/psql\b.*(-U|--username[= ]?)\s*postgres\b/` → "superuser probes bypass FORCE RLS and false-green; use app_api or the rls_verify MCP" (T5 pitfall); `/tauri\s+signer\s+generate/` → "updater keys are human-managed tier-0 secrets"; `/dangerousInsecureTransportProtocol/`

**pretool-write-guard.mjs** (matcher Edit|Write|MultiEdit; CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR-relative resolution carried verbatim):
- PROTECTED (root-anchored, HARNESS_ALLOW_SELF_EDIT=1 escape): `^tools/(harness\.config|validate|check-[^/]+|build-check|run-rust-gates)\.mjs$`, `^tests/rls/run-rls\.mjs$`, `^eslint\.config\.mjs$`, `^eslint/harness\.eslint\.mjs$`, `^biome\.jsonc$`, `^knip\.json$`, `^\.dependency-cruiser\.cjs$`, `^tsconfig\.json$`, `^packages/config/tsconfig[^/]*\.json$`, `^pnpm-workspace\.yaml$` (catalog pins!), `^vitest\.config\.ts$`, `^lefthook\.yml$`, `^commitlint\.config\.mjs$`, `^\.github/workflows/`, `^deny\.toml$`, `^rust-toolchain\.toml$`, `^apps/desktop/src-tauri/identity\.lock\.json$`, `^\.gitleaks\.toml$`
- content checks (concatenate content/new_string/edits[].new_string, path-heuristic scoped):
  - privileged-DSN ban (service_role analog): `/MIGRATOR_DATABASE_URL|postgres(ql)?:\/\/[^'"\s]*:[^'"\s]*@/` in apps/** or packages/** outside packages/schema/scripts/** and tests/** seed helpers
  - GUC discipline: `/set_config\(\s*['"]app\.[^'"]+['"],[^)]*,\s*false\s*\)/` and `/\bSET\s+(?!LOCAL\b)(SESSION\s+)?app\./i` → "transaction-local only; session SET leaks identity across pooled connections" (T5r2)
  - tauri.conf.json / capabilities/*.json edits denied when new content adds `'unsafe-inline'|'unsafe-eval'` to csp, a `"remote"` key, `"windows": ["*"]`, `$HOME` or bare `"**"` scopes, `"use": "brownfield"`, `"downloadBootstrapper"`, `"withGlobalTauri": true`, or when a tauri.conf.json write omits `"csp"` entirely (T1r0/r1/r4/r5, T13r3)
  - Vite secret inlining: `/VITE_[A-Z_]*(SECRET|PRIVATE|SIGNING|KEY|TOKEN|PASSWORD)/` anywhere (T1r9 / GHSA-2rcp-jvr4-r259)
  - vitest v3 muscle memory: deny creating `vitest.workspace.*` or content matching `/defineWorkspace\(/` (T3 pitfall)
  - raw IPC: `from '@tauri-apps/api/core'` outside `apps/desktop/src/ipc/` (T1r3)
  - migration hygiene: new `packages/schema/drizzle/*.sql` containing `/DROP\s+(TABLE|COLUMN)|ALTER\s+COLUMN\s+\w+\s+TYPE|SET\s+NOT\s+NULL/i` denied unless sibling `docs/adr/destructive/<file>.md` exists in the same tree (T11r3); DML (`UPDATE|DELETE|INSERT\s+INTO.*SELECT`) in migration files denied → "backfills live in packages/schema/backfills/" (T11r4)
  - positive requirement (DAL server-only analog): new `apps/server/src/dal/*.ts` must import the transaction context (`from '../db/context'` providing withUser) — scaffold stubs satisfy it, mirroring the import-'server-only' trick
  - carried: `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` ban

**posttool-fast-check.mjs**: carried verbatim (biome check --write on the single file, always exit 0); extended: if file is .rs and rustfmt on PATH, run `rustfmt` on it (still never blocks).

**posttool-source-check.mjs**: carried logic verbatim (disk re-read, comment-line skip, 3-line lookback, exit 2). New DECISION regex, kept in lockstep with tools/check-sources.mjs (dual-file doctrine): `pgPolicy(|FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY|security_invoker|set_config(|current_setting(|jwtVerify(|createRemoteJWKSet(|clockTolerance|hnsw|iterative_scan|WITH RECURSIVE|lock_timeout|statement_timeout|maxRetries|timeoutMs|rateLimit|temperature:|seed:`.

**stop-validate-gate.mjs**: carried verbatim — dynamic import of STOP_HOOK_STEPS with warn-and-fallback, execSync maxBuffer 64MB (vite/cargo output), last-4000-chars per failure, stop_hook_active re-entry header, CLAUDE_CODE_STOP_HOOK_BLOCK_CAP bound.

## settings.json (Layer 1)
- allow: pnpm validate/typecheck/lint/knip/arch/build/test:*, pnpm exec vitest/biome/tsc/eslint/depcruise/playwright, node tools/*, `drizzle-kit generate|check|studio`, `docker compose -f docker/db.compose.yml up|down|ps|logs`, `cargo check|clippy|fmt|test`, `pnpm tauri dev`, `pnpm tauri build --debug`; MCP: corpus_search, rls_verify; WebFetch domains: v2.tauri.app, tauri.app, vite.dev, react.dev, hono.dev, orm.drizzle.team, zod.dev, postgresql.org, github.com/pgvector, learn.microsoft.com, developer.mozilla.org, nodejs.org, owasp.org, code.claude.com
- ask: git push, `drizzle-kit migrate` (non-scratch), release `tauri build`, signing/minisign ops, `systemctl`/systemd ops, docker against non-local hosts
- deny (carried + re-pointed): curl/wget, rm -rf, force-push, reset --hard, `Bash(* MIGRATOR_DATABASE_URL *)`, `Bash(* drizzle-kit push *)`, Read .env globs + `Read(./secrets/**)` + `Read(~/.ssh/**)`, Edit/Write on .claude/hooks/**, settings*.json, .mcp.json, .harness/** — verbatim (write-guard exempts .claude/ so Layer 1 must protect it — carried gotcha)
- defaultMode acceptEdits, disableBypassPermissionsMode "disable", env CLAUDE_CODE_STOP_HOOK_BLOCK_CAP=8, statusline wired — all verbatim.

Layer 3 remains validate.mjs --min-floor in CI. All three layers ship together (documented invariant).

## Skill: authoring-vertical-slice (structure carried: locked order, lazy references, idempotent scaffolder)
Locked order: schema (packages/schema) → migration generate → RLS policies + isolation test → DAL (apps/server/src/dal) → zod-openapi route → contract regen (openapi.json green) → desktop screen (TanStack Query, keyboard registry, ErrorBoundary, screen-manifest entry) → tests → provenance → gate. references/: schema-migration-rls.md, dal-route-contracts.md, desktop-screen-a11y-perf.md, tests.md, llm-slice.md (read only when the slice touches extraction/eval). scripts/scaffold-slice.mjs re-pointed to monorepo paths, preserving both idempotency tricks (migration-file scan for *_<feature>.sql instead of existsSync; stubs never contain banned literal tokens so later full-file writes aren't denied).

## Rules (always-loaded; no paths: scoping — carried lesson)
- security-invariants.md — rewritten: never trust unverified Entra tokens (verify iss/aud/tid/sig via JWKS); MIGRATOR DSN banned outside migration tooling; FORCE RLS + SET LOCAL GUC per request; Tauri capabilities gate windows, not users — authorize in the DAL; DTOs not raw rows; no tokens in webview localStorage (refresh tokens live in the Rust host); security_invoker on all views; no rm -rf/force-push/.env reads
- provenance.md — near-verbatim (decision-site list updated)
- rsc-client-split.md → client-server-boundary.md — apps/desktop never imports drizzle/pg/server internals (talks only to the typed hc client); Hono middleware is optimistic, the DAL is the authorization boundary; hard enforcement is depcruise+write-guard, this rule is advisory

CLAUDE.md kernel rewritten <200 lines (advisory-only doctrine stated; monorepo map, pnpm catalog, commands, validate contract, security invariants, Tauri identity/CSP gotchas, spec-first governance). statusline.mjs and settings.local.json.example carried (env: DATABASE_URL, MIGRATOR_DATABASE_URL, ENTRA_TENANT_ID/CLIENT_ID/API_AUDIENCE, AUTH_MODE, LLAMA_BASE_URL, CORPUS_INDEX_URL).

## Installer changes
- **detect.mjs (rewritten)**: bootstrap = no package.json (empty-dir check carried). ## template/stack — "curriculum-notes", the notes-app equivalent
A minimal end-to-end vertical slice through every layer of the monorepo, proving the whole doctrine with zero external services:

- **packages/config**: tsconfig.base.json (full max-strict flag set), tsconfig.vite.json (module preserve, noEmit), tsconfig.node.json (nodenext, erasableSyntaxOnly) — T2r1.

risks: Stop-gate latency creep: type-aware ESLint + tsc -b + vite build across five workspaces could exceed ~3 min on cold caches, tempting users to hollow the gate — mitigate by measuring wall time in selftest with a budget assertion, keeping browser/WDIO/mutation lanes out of the default chain, and documenting the fast path (incremental tsbuildinfo, biome-first ordering).

Rust self-skip could mask local breakage for agents without cargo (red only appears in CI) — mitigated by the printed SKIP, the CI fail-closed rule, and the selftest windows leg, but it is a real parity weakening vs the original's always-runnable gates.

drizzle-kit is mid-1.0-rc churn: unpinned upgrades change migration layout and break check-contract-drift and the destructive-DDL guard — pin exactly via catalog and cover with a Renovate cooldown; same for tauri-cli (signCommand file-set regressions, tauri#13991).

openapi.json regeneration in the validate chain requires importing the server route tree side-effect-free; any agent adding top-level env reads or DB connects to route modules breaks the contracts gate confusingly — enforce lazy env via lint and document the failure signature.

Write-guard content regexes on tauri.conf.json/capabilities and migration SQL have a higher false-positive surface than the original's (JSON edits are structural, regex is textual) — mitigate by making every deny message name the exact override path, and keep check-tauri-policy (structural JSON parse) the authoritative layer.

Windows CI economics: the selftest windows leg + template desktop-windows job burn 2x minutes; if path filters are wrong the repo either exhausts quota or silently never builds the .exe on PRs — the nightly schedule is the backstop and must exist from day one (cache eviction after 7 days).

The isolation pattern + dist-isolation in the bootstrap app adds Vite/Tauri wiring complexity that could break bootstrap-green on Tauri minor bumps — if it destabilizes selftest, fall back to shipping brownfield with the tauri-policy assert relaxed to a warning and isolation promoted in the strict tier (decision recorded in an ADR).

AUTH_MODE=local-jwks is a test seam in the auth boundary: a misconfiguration shipping local-jwks to production would be catastrophic — enforce a boot-time fatal when NODE_ENV=production and AUTH_MODE!=entra, plus a hygiene leak pattern for the test JWKS key.

pnpm catalog + project references + knip workspaces are three parallel dependency topologies agents can desynchronize — ship the references-sync check (workspaces-to-typescript-project-references --check) inside check-contract-drift or lint, or drift will produce confusing type errors.

FLOOR/config lockstep is still hand-maintained duplication (by design); with 10 floor steps vs the original's 7 the drift risk grows — add a selftest assertion that every FLOOR name exists in VALIDATE_STEPS with an identical command string.

perspective: World-class quality bar — owner of gate CONTENT. My job was to turn ~140 verified research recommendations into the strongest mechanically-enforced quality system that still bootstraps green and keeps the Stop hook fast. Every major recommendation below is explicitly dispositioned as: default gate, opt-in module, CI-only, doctrine doc, or rejected (with reason). Ruthless runtime discipline: the default Stop chain targets ≤45s warm; anything slower is CI-only or a module.
summary:
tauri-postgres-agent-harness is a byte-level architectural sibling of next-supabase-agent-harness — same installer (init/update/doctor/enable), manifest hashing with owned/seeded/config ownership, merge-never-clobber retrofit, dual npx+plugin channels, dotless template storage, three-layer tamper evidence (settings deny → write-guard PROTECTED → CI --min-floor), and the config-single-source-of-truth gate chain consumed identically by Stop hook, `pnpm validate`, and CI. What changes is the CONTENT: the gate chain, hooks, agents, and reference app are rebuilt for a pnpm monorepo shipping a Tauri 2 Windows desktop client (React 19 + Vite + TS max-strict + Tailwind v4 + shadcn) against a Hono/Node 22 server with a server-only Drizzle DAL over self-hosted Postgres 16 + pgvector under FORCE RLS with Entra OIDC and request-scoped session-variable identity, plus llama.cpp-backed LLM pipeline/eval packages.

The design thesis: this stack has five trust boundaries the old harness never had — the Tauri capability/CSP/IPC surface, the Rust host, the desktop/server package boundary (no `import 'server-only'` exists in Vite), the two-ecosystem supply chain (npm + crates), and the LLM pipeline — and each gets a dedicated fast deterministic gate (tauri-policy JSON lint, cargo fmt/check with clippy+deny+audit in CI, desktop-purity dist scan + dependency-cruiser law, dual-lockfile scanners, prompt-lockfile + replay-only eval regression). The headline security gate remains a real-Postgres RLS isolation suite (docker Postgres 16 + pgvector, seeded positive controls, catalog assertions for FORCE/per-op policies/security_invoker views/role hygiene) with the sibling's skip-local/fail-closed-CI asymmetry. Heavy fidelity lanes (Windows signing+installer smoke, WDIO-on-.exe, perf budgets, mutation, crash reporting, pgBackRest restore drills, macOS) are ten opt-in modules so a fresh install passes every default gate immediately — proven, as before, by a selftest bootstrap matrix plus a new deliberate-violation canary job that asserts every gate can actually fail.

Hard-reject: Next.js apps, non-pnpm monorepos, and repos whose workspace names collide with apps/desktop|apps/server without --force-map (every gate glob assumes the canonical workspace names; document the remap knob in harness.config paths block instead of guessing).
- **merge-never-clobber**: package.json merge now runs per-workspace (root + apps/* + packages/*) plus pnpm-workspace.yaml package-glob union; CONFLICTABLE extended to vite.config.ts, tauri.conf.json, drizzle.config.ts, vitest.config.ts, dependency-cruiser.cjs, biome.jsonc, eslint.config.mjs, lefthook.yml, deny.toml, rust-toolchain.toml (conflicts parked as <base>.harness.<ext> siblings, exactly as today).
- **placeholders.mjs**: keep PROJECT_NAME/PROJECT_SLUG/GITHUB_OWNER/SECURITY_OWNERS/DEFAULT_BRANCH; add BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER (default com.{{GITHUB_OWNER}}.{{PROJECT_SLUG}}, ≤30-char lint), ENTRA_TENANT_ID, ENTRA_CLIENT_ID, API_ORIGIN, POSTGRES_DB, WINDOWS_PUBLISHER. Declaration order stays load-bearing (derived defaults). Bidirectional closure gate in hygiene.mjs unchanged. render() still ignores ${{ }}.
- **manifest/layout**: hashes over post-render content unchanged. owned = tools/, .claude/, .github/workflows/, lint/arch configs, deny.toml; seeded = apps/**, packages/** app code, AGENTS.md, docker-compose.yml, migrations, prompts; config (hash-tracked) = tools/harness.config.mjs, tauri.conf.json, identity.lock.json, knip.json. RENAMES adds npmrc→.npmrc, node-version→.node-version, squawk.toml→.squawk.toml.
- **doctor** new probes: cargo --version + rustup target (error if absent: "install prerequisites, see docs/harness/prereqs"), docker reachability (warn), hooks wired in settings.json, harness.config exports non-empty, tauri.conf identity == identity.lock, prompts.lock consistent, unrendered {{tokens}}, HARNESS_HOOK_VERSION present in every hook and bumped-on-change (R12.8).
- **enable**: same file-drop vs uncomment-a-step split; enable prints the HARNESS_ALLOW_SELF_EDIT hint for gate-* modules. TIERS: core=[], standard=[gate-a11y-deep, gate-perf-budget, mutation], strict=all modules.
- **License handling (NEW)**: repo is Apache-2.0; template/** carries 0BSD SPDX headers via REUSE.toml; init writes the consumer's chosen LICENSE and strips harness headers — unit-tested, and instantiation CI greps output for harness copyright strings (R12.0, P12.3).

## Harness-own CI (.github/workflows)
- **selftest.yml** (the release acceptance test): jobs — installer-unit (syntax/hygiene/placeholders/lifecycle/merge); hook-contracts (stdin JSON fixtures → exact exit codes incl. malformed-JSON fail-loud); **canary (NEW, R12.7)**: instantiate → assert green → inject a type error + a biome violation + a protected-path write attempt → assert validate exits non-zero AND stop-validate-gate exits exactly 2 AND the write-guard denies → revert → assert green again in the same job (grep-verifies the injection actually landed); **bootstrap matrix** (ubuntu, Node 22/24): init --yes → pnpm install → rustup + cargo check warm-up → Postgres16+pgvector service container → `node tools/validate.mjs` green out of the box → `node tests/rls/run-rls.mjs` must ACTUALLY run (assert "[rls] OK", and separately assert the fail-closed path exits 1 with "failing closed" when the DB is stopped) → vitest; **bootstrap-windows leg**: init → pnpm install → `pnpm tauri build --debug` proves the .exe path compiles — runs on PRs touching template/stack/** or installer/**, plus nightly (Windows minutes are 2x-billed, R4.1/P4.1); **retrofit**: plant conflicting configs/scripts, assert non-clobber from the JSON report; placeholder-residue grep (`{{[A-Z_]+}}`) + dotless-rename assertion (P12.7).
- **hygiene.yml**: leak patterns re-targeted (new org identifiers, Entra client-secret shapes, non-local Postgres DSNs, minisign/`.pfx`/thumbprints, TAURI_SIGNING literals, .gguf absolute paths); fails if template/ missing.
- **lint.yml**: actionlint + zizmor pedantic over own workflows AND template workflows staged into .fixtures (dotless-storage trick preserved).
- **release.yml**: CHANGELOG `## [x.y.z]` section gate → npm pack → attest-build-provenance → gh release; npm-pack content test asserts dotless paths ship and .claude/ survives.
- **scorecard.yml** + **reuse.yml** (NEW: fsfe/reuse-action lint as required check).
All actions SHA-pinned + harden-runner; Renovate config:best-practices with minimumReleaseAge cooldown (R4.5).

## Template CI shipped into projects (dotless under template/base/github/workflows/)
- **quality-gate.yml**: ubuntu — `node tools/validate.mjs --min-floor` with HARNESS_REQUIRE_TOOLCHAINS=1; runtime job (Postgres16+pgvector service → migrate → run-rls fail-closed); cargo lane (fmt/clippy -D warnings/nextest + capabilities-vs-fresh-schema ajv); browser lane (Vitest Browser Mode + axe component scans); playwright fast lane (mocked-IPC + inventory diff + degraded-network); integration lane (testcontainers: SSE abort, shutdown, readyz); coverage ratchet. Every job: timeout-minutes, concurrency-cancel, harden-runner (audit on any windows job — block unsupported there, P4.3).
- **rust-security.yml**: cargo-deny + cargo audit --deny warnings, PR + nightly cron.
- **migration-safety.yml** (path-filtered on drizzle/**): Squawk error-level + destructive-ADR coupling + append-only diff vs merge-base.
- **api-contract.yml**: openapi regenerate-diff + oasdiff breaking + MIN_CLIENT_VERSION coupling rule.
- **codeql.yml** (javascript-typescript, rust, actions), **osv-scan.yml** (pnpm-lock.yaml + Cargo.lock), **gitleaks.yml** (binary direct, not the action), **actions-lint.yml** (actionlint + zizmor).
No Windows job in default template CI — everything Windows-shaped lives in the ci-windows-* modules (cost + bootstrap-green).

## template/stack — the "notes desktop" app (the notes-app equivalent)
A deliberately tiny, fully wired vertical slice: a keyboard-first, owner-scoped **notes list** desktop app with one stubbed AI action ("summarize note" through the InferenceProvider port using FakeProvider), so every default gate has a real, non-vacuous subject on day one.

- **packages/config**: tsconfig.base.json (full max-strict flag set: noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, noPropertyAccessFromIndexSignature, verbatimModuleSyntax, isolatedModules…) + vite/node overlays (module preserve vs nodenext + erasableSyntaxOnly). ESM-only, "type":"module" everywhere (P2.6).
- **packages/schema**: `notes` pgTable (id, owner_sub, title, body, embedding vector(N) nullable, provenance columns NOT NULL on the ai_summaries example table with confidence CHECK) + pgPolicy per operation keyed on `(SELECT current_setting('app.user_sub', true))` + pgRole declarations; drizzle-zod DTOs; EMBEDDING_DIM constant (single source of truth for the pgvector dimension contract); exported AppType consumed by the desktop's pre-compiled hcWithType wrapper (P2.4). drizzle/ contains: 0000_roles_and_grants.sql (app_api NOSUPERUSER NOBYPASSRLS, migrator owner, explicit GRANTs + ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES — encoding the sibling's dropped-public-grants lesson), 0001_notes.sql with ENABLE+FORCE RLS + four policies + lock/statement-timeout preamble.
- **apps/server**: Hono 4 + @hono/zod-openapi routes (notes CRUD, /api/meta version handshake, /healthz + /readyz), committed openapi.json; jose auth middleware verifying against a configurable issuer — in dev/test a **local JWKS stub + token minter** (tests/setup) so bootstrap needs no Entra tenant, with ENTRA_* env switching to real Entra in production; skew middleware (x-client-version → 426 problem+json) registered globally with the route-coverage introspection test; DAL with withUser(sub, roles, fn) doing txn-scoped set_config; pino logger.ts with redaction config; SSE demo route with onAbort propagation; infra/api.service systemd unit (hardening block, LoadCredentialEncrypted pattern, READY-after-DB-ping).
- **apps/desktop**: Vite + React 19 (React Compiler exact-pinned) + Tailwind v4 + shadcn; Tauri 2 (≥2.11.1) shell: strict CSP (connect-src = ipc: + http://ipc.localhost + {{API_ORIGIN}} only), isolation pattern with a dependency-free dist-isolation hook, ONE capability file for the main window (no wildcards), identity.lock.json, NSIS/perMachine/offlineInstaller bundle config, decorations true, updater compiled out (managed flavor default), build.rs embedding the longPathAware manifest; one `#[tauri::command]` (app_version) exported through tauri-specta with committed bindings.ts; src-tauri Cargo.toml with [lints] workspace = true (unsafe forbid, unwrap/expect/panic deny, print/dbg deny) + tracing/tauri-plugin-log spine + dirty-shutdown sentinel; SPA: virtualized notes list, shortcut registry package, TanStack Query, 426 update-required and offline/degraded states (aria-live), one *.test.tsx (jsdom) + one *.browser.test.tsx with a direct axe-core scan.
- **packages/importer**: minimal xlsx→notes importer with one fast-check model-based round-trip property test.
- **packages/eval**: InferenceProvider/EmbeddingProvider ports + LlamaCppProvider (throws when EVAL_MODE=replay) + Fake + Replay adapters; one summarize.prompt.md with frontmatter {id, version, model, params, schemaRef, model_license, model_license_url} + prompts.lock.json; one recorded fixture + baselines/summarize.json + a scorer; vitest setup installs undici MockAgent disableNetConnect (structural hermeticity, P8.4); adapter contract-test suite.
- **docker-compose.yml**: pgvector/pgvector:pg16 pinned by digest; optional commented llama-server profile (--parallel 1 eval profile) pinned by image digest.

## What it must prove (bootstrap-green = every default gate exercised, none vacuous)
1. `node tools/validate.mjs` green in ≤~45s warm, including cargo fmt/check on the real crate and vite build + purity scan on the real dist.
2. `node tests/rls/run-rls.mjs` against the compose DB actually runs and passes with a seeded positive control (A sees own rows) — and exits 1 "failing closed" in CI when the DB is absent; the catalog gate sees a real FORCE-RLS table, real per-op policies, real app_api role assertions.
3. Contract gates have real subjects: committed openapi.json regenerates byte-identical; hc<AppType> compiles in the desktop; the skew middleware coverage test walks a real route table; version-sync sees three real version sites.
4. Tauri policy gate sees a real capability file + CSP + identity.lock; cargo check compiles the real ACL.
5. Eval gates run on real artifacts with zero network: prompt lockfile verifies, replay regression compares real recordings to real baselines, LlamaCppProvider provably refuses live calls in CI.
6. Provenance: every decision site in the scaffold (policies, jose options, clockTolerance, GUC calls, CSP, EMBEDDING_DIM) carries a resolving `// SOURCE:` so check-sources passes non-vacuously and /verify-citations returns CLEAN.
7. The .exe path compiles: selftest's windows leg runs `pnpm tauri build --debug` on the scaffold.

risks: Cold-start cargo latency: the first cargo check compiles Tauri's dependency tree for minutes, so a Stop hook firing before the documented `pnpm bootstrap` warm-up looks hung — mitigate with run-if warm-cache detection messaging and a selftest assertion on warm-chain wall time.

drizzle-kit is mid-1.0.0-rc churn (no down migrations, rename prompts never fire in CI, migration file format can shift): the migrations drift gate must pin drizzle-kit EXACTLY and Renovate cooldown it, or the gate flaps on tool upgrades (P11.5, P5.3).

openapi.json regenerate-diff is only sound if generation is deterministic — key ordering, schema $ref naming, and Zod version drift can all produce spurious diffs; stable stringify + single pinned zod via pnpm.overrides is load-bearing (P6.6).

Bootstrap-green under max-strict is a curation burden: exactOptionalPropertyTypes and strictTypeChecked fight shadcn/radix types; every template dependency bump can break the green promise, so selftest must run on Renovate PRs too (P2.2).

Windows CI economics: the selftest windows leg and both ci-windows modules burn 2x-billed minutes; path-filter/nightly discipline must be enforced by the workflow-lint script or an active repo exhausts quotas in days (P4.1).

Skip-local/fail-closed-CI asymmetry now covers THREE toolchains (docker/Postgres, cargo, node) — a bug in run-if or the CI env flag silently false-greens the exact gates that matter most; hook-contract and selftest tests must cover both directions for each (P12.2 class).

Ecosystem velocity vs agent training data: Vitest 4 (no defineWorkspace), @wdio/tauri-service, tauri-specta v2, TS 6/7 split — agents will regenerate removed APIs; the write-guard bans help but every pinned tool needs a documented upgrade runbook or gates rot (P3.0, P2.0).

knip and eslint-boundaries false positives on dynamic references (Rust-invoked command strings, GBNF assets, prompt files) tempt gate-weakening; pre-seeded knip.json entries must ship correct or the first agent turn stalls on a protected file it cannot edit (P2.7).

Two-ecosystem advisory noise: nightly cargo-audit/osv crons will go red on unrelated CVEs; without the expiring-justification convention in deny.toml and the allowlist files, teams learn to ignore red — which is worse than no cron (P1.5).

The dev JWKS stub is a security-shaped footgun: if the Entra/dev issuer switch is env-based, a misconfigured production deploy could accept stub tokens — the config loader must hard-fail when NODE_ENV=production and the stub issuer is configured, with a unit test proving it.

## Default VALIDATE_STEPS (tools/harness.config.mjs — ordered cheap→expensive, break on first failure)

| # | step | command | catches | warm runtime | encodes |
|---|------|---------|---------|--------------|---------|
| 1 | `format` | `pnpm exec biome check .` | formatting + fast correctness; Biome is SOLE formatter (Prettier dropped — kills the sibling's ordering hazard) | 1–2s | TS-monorepo rec 5 (single-owner linter matrix) |
| 2 | `types` | `pnpm exec tsc -b` | max-strict flags from packages/config base (noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, verbatimModuleSyntax…), project-references topology, cross-package contract drift (hc\<AppType\>) | 2–5s incremental (15–30s cold) | TS recs 1–3, 8 (contracts drift is a build failure) |
| 3 | `lint` | `pnpm exec eslint . --max-warnings 0` | strictTypeChecked via projectService; jsx-a11y strict; boundaries; custom invariants: jose only in auth/, pino only via logger.ts, `invoke()` only in src/ipc/, throw-ban in services/importer, no drizzle/pg imports in desktop, no `@tauri-apps/plugin-http` in SPA | 10–30s | TS rec 4; Hono recs 1,5,7; meta rec 2; enterprise-net rec 2 |
| 4 | `provenance` | `node tools/check-sources.mjs` | `// SOURCE:` at decision sites; regex kept in LOCKSTEP with posttool-source-check.mjs: `pgPolicy\(|FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY|current_setting\(|jwtVerify\(|createRemoteJWKSet\(|clockTolerance|maxRetries|timeoutMs|rateLimit|hnsw|WITH RECURSIVE|temperature:` | <1s | provenance doctrine (parity) |
| 5 | `tauri-policy` | `node tools/check-tauri-policy.mjs` | pure-JSON asserts on tauri.conf.json + capabilities/*.json + identity.lock.json: CSP non-null, no unsafe-inline/eval in script-src, connect-src allowlist regex, no `remote` key, no `windows:["*"]`, no `$HOME/**`/bare `**` scopes, freezePrototype=true, withGlobalTauri=false, targets=["nsis"], installMode=perMachine, webviewInstallMode=offlineInstaller (explicit), decorations≠false, identity fields == identity.lock.json | <1s | Tauri-security recs 1,2,5; packaging recs 1,4,8; UX rec 5 (NVDA); enterprise rec 4 — ~10 research recs in one deterministic gate |
| 6 | `schema-rls` | `node tools/check-schema-rls.mjs` (tsx-imports packages/schema) | every exported pgTable carries pgPolicy covering SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE or sits in reviewable `rls-exempt.json`; migrations grep for `FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY` per table | 2–3s | PG recs 1,4 |
| 7 | `migrations-drift` | `node tools/check-migrations-drift.mjs` (`drizzle-kit generate` to tmp → fail if new migration emitted; + `drizzle-kit check`) | schema/migration desync, journal corruption; makes `push` unnecessary | 2–4s | PG rec 5 |
| 8 | `contracts` | `node tools/check-openapi-drift.mjs` (tsx-renders OpenAPI doc, canonical-sorted diff vs committed openapi.json) | route changes without spec recommit | 2–4s | Hono rec 2; lifecycle rec 8 |
| 9 | `dead-code` | `pnpm exec knip --strict` | unused files/exports/deps across workspaces | 5–15s | TS rec 6 |
| 10 | `architecture` | `pnpm exec depcruise apps packages --config .dependency-cruiser.cjs` | desktop→drizzle/pg/server-internals ban; only schema imports drizzle-zod; only eval adapters touch LLM HTTP; no-circular | 3–8s | TS rec 7; Hono rec 1; LLM rec 3 |
| 11 | `rust-check` | `node tools/check-rust.mjs` → `cargo check --workspace --locked` with a content-hash stamp in `.harness/rust-check.ok` (stamp hit = instant pass; stamp ignored when CI=true; cargo missing → warn-skip locally, FAIL in CI) | Rust compile errors, unknown capability identifiers (tauri-build hard-fails on them) | <1s stamped / 5–60s changed / minutes cold | testing rec 5; Tauri-security rec 7 (cargo check regenerates + validates ACL) |
| 12 | `build` | `pnpm --filter desktop exec vite build && node tools/check-bundle-size.mjs` | bundler failures, per-chunk byte budgets (generous 2× headroom, versioned JSON), plus a dist/ grep for `TAURI_SIGNING|VITE_[A-Z_]*(KEY|SECRET|TOKEN)` catching the GHSA-2rcp-jvr4-r259 class | 5–15s | UX rec 9; Tauri-security pitfall 7 |

## STOP_HOOK_STEPS (direct invocation, never package.json script names)
1. `['validate', 'node tools/validate.mjs']`
2. `['rls-isolation', 'node tools/rls/run-rls.mjs']` — probes DATABASE_URL/compose default; unreachable locally → honest green skip; unreachable in CI with migrations present → exit 1 "failing closed"; reachable → migrate as migrator, seed A/B, run catalog SQL asserts + behavioral isolation (seeded positive control: A sees own N rows; A sees 0 of B; cross-tenant UPDATE/DELETE touch 0; smuggled INSERT rejected)
3. `['unit', 'pnpm exec vitest run --project unit-node --project unit-dom']` — 5–10s

**Total warm turn: ~45–90s.** Everything heavier (tauri build, WDIO, browser mode, mutation, live eval) is CI-only or an opt-in module — per testing pitfall 8 ("20-minute Stop chains get gamed").

## CI floor (`validate.mjs --min-floor`)
FLOOR = verbatim duplicates of `format, types, lint, provenance, tauri-policy, dead-code, architecture, rust-check, build` (identical names AND commands). `schema-rls`, `migrations-drift`, `contracts` are project-shaped (self-skip when packages/schema / apps/server absent, e.g. on partial retrofits) so they stay config-level, mirroring how the sibling excludes route-boundaries/aliveness from its floor.

## Per-gate false-positive story (bootstrap-green requirement)
format/lint: generated files (drizzle meta/migrations, openapi.json, dist) excluded in config; single-owner rule test prevents Biome/ESLint double-diagnostics. types: incremental cache; exactOptionalPropertyTypes third-party fights are fixed locally, never by weakening (tsconfig write-guarded). tauri-policy: reads only committed JSON — deterministic; legit identity changes go through human-gated identity.lock.json edit. schema-rls: rls-exempt.json is reviewable data. migrations-drift: drizzle-kit EXACT-pinned (1.0-rc churn is a known hazard). contracts: canonical key-sorted serialization kills ordering noise. dead-code: knip entry globs cover vite/tauri/eval/scripts entrypoints; `knip --fix` bash-guard-denied. rust-check: content-hash stamp can't go stale-green (hash covers src-tauri/** + Cargo.lock); missing toolchain is a warn-skip locally with a doctor probe. build: budgets at 2× current size, bumps are a one-line reviewed JSON edit. rls-isolation: skip-before-schema / fail-closed-in-CI asymmetry preserved exactly from the sibling.
hooks_and_guards:
## Hook inventory (all import `.claude/hooks/lib/hookio.mjs` — ported VERBATIM: pass()=exit 0; denyTool()=exit 0 + permissionDecision:"deny" JSON for PreToolUse; block()=stderr + exit 2 for PostToolUse/Stop; fail-loud uncaughtException handlers so an internal error can never read as "allow")

### pretool-bash-guard.mjs (PreToolUse, matcher Bash) — [regex, message] tripwire table
Kept verbatim: `rm -rf` (flag permutations), `git push --force|-f|--force-with-lease`, `git reset --hard`, fork bomb, `.env*` reads.
New stack rules:
- `/drizzle-kit\s+push/` → "push is banned; generate + migrate only (no history, silent column drops)"
- `/knip\s+(--fix|--fix-type)/` → "agent auto-deletion is banned; fix code or the reviewed knip.json"
- `/\b(cargo\s+update|pnpm\s+(up|update)\b)/` → "dependency bumps are Renovate's job; needs explicit human task"
- `/TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY|trusted-signing|--pfx|\.pfx\b/` → "signing material never transits an agent shell"
- `/postgres(ql)?:\/\/[^\s]*migrator/` → "migrator DSN only inside the migration runner"
- `/DROP\s+(TABLE|SCHEMA|DATABASE|ROLE)/i` in psql/one-liner contexts → deny
- `/vitest\s+.*\.only|--update-?snapshots/` piped patterns left to lint (avoid FP).
Header keeps the "tripwire, not sandbox" honesty note; settings.json deny-list remains Layer 1.

### pretool-write-guard.mjs (PreToolUse, matcher Edit|Write|MultiEdit)
**Layer-2 PROTECTED list (root-anchored regexes, CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR-relative resolution preserved; deny unless HARNESS_ALLOW_SELF_EDIT=1):**
`^tools/(validate|harness\.config|check-[a-z-]+)\.mjs$`, `^tools/rls/run-rls\.mjs$`, `^tools/mcp/.*`, `^\.dependency-cruiser\.cjs$`, `^eslint\.config\.mjs$`, `^packages/config/(tsconfig\..*\.json|harness\.eslint\.mjs)$`, `^biome\.jsonc$`, `^tsconfig(\..+)?\.json$`, `^knip\.json$`, `^lefthook\.yml$`, `^commitlint\.config\.mjs$`, `^\.github/workflows/.*`, `^apps/desktop/src-tauri/identity\.lock\.json$`, `^packages/schema/drizzle/.*` (append-only: edits to EXISTING migration files denied; new files allowed), `^\.harness/.*`, `^rust-toolchain\.toml$`, `^pnpm-workspace\.yaml$`.
(.claude/** stays exempt here — protected by settings.json deny, Layer 1. Both layers or a gap opens.)
**Content checks on candidate new content (content/new_string/edits[].new_string union):**
- tsconfig*: reject removing/false-ing a protected flag (jq-style diff vs baseline list) — TS rec 2
- `tauri.conf.json` / `capabilities/*.json` (NOT blanket-protected — legit edits happen): deny if new content deletes `csp`, adds `unsafe-inline|unsafe-eval` to script-src, adds `"remote"`, `"windows": ["*"]`, `$HOME` or bare `**` scopes, sets `assetProtocol.enable: true` without scope, `withGlobalTauri: true`, `decorations: false`, `webviewInstallMode` → `downloadBootstrapper`, or changes identifier/publisher/pubkey vs identity.lock.json
- any source file: deny `set_config(` with third arg `false`; `SET app.` without `LOCAL`; `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`; `VITE_[A-Z_]*(KEY|SECRET|TOKEN|PRIVATE)`; new `vitest.workspace.*` file or `defineWorkspace(` import (Vitest-3 muscle-memory trap); `eslint-disable` without ` -- reason`
- path-contextual (isDesktop = `^apps/desktop/src/`): deny `from 'drizzle-orm'|'pg'`, `@azure/msal`, `@tauri-apps/plugin-http`, `localStorage.setItem` with /token/i, `invoke(` outside `^apps/desktop/src/ipc/`
- path-contextual (isServer = `^apps/server/src/` minus auth/): deny `from 'jose'` outside `src/auth/`; `console.` outside logger module; `process.env.` outside `src/env.ts`
- `.gguf` path/hash added without sibling `model_license` field (governance rec 4)

### posttool-fast-check.mjs (PostToolUse, non-blocking, always exit 0)
`biome check --write` on the single changed JS/TS file (verbatim); NEW: `rustfmt --edition 2021 <file>` for `.rs` when rustfmt present (still non-blocking). Heavy checks stay on Stop.

### posttool-source-check.mjs (PostToolUse, blocking exit 2)
Verbatim mechanics (disk re-read, comment-line skip, 3-line lookback for `// SOURCE:`); DECISION regex swapped to the stack set listed in the gate-chain section and kept in LOCKSTEP with tools/check-sources.mjs (dual-file pattern preserved, header comments say so).

### stop-validate-gate.mjs (Stop, matcher '')
Verbatim port: dynamic import of STOP_HOOK_STEPS with warn-and-fallback-to-core (never skip), execSync maxBuffer 64MB (cargo/vite output), last-4000-chars failure surfaces, stop_hook_active re-entry header, CLAUDE_CODE_STOP_HOOK_BLOCK_CAP bound.

### settings.json (Layer 1)
- **deny**: Edit/Write of `.claude/hooks/**`, `settings*.json`, `.mcp.json`, `.harness/**`; Read of `.env*`, `~/.ssh/**`, `**/*.pfx`, `**/*.key`; Bash `curl *`, `wget *`, `rm -rf *`, `git push --force*`, `git reset --hard*`, `* drizzle-kit push *`, `* TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY *`
- **allow**: pnpm build/test/lint scripts, `pnpm exec vitest|tsc|biome|eslint|knip|depcruise`, `node tools/*`, `cargo check|clippy|fmt|test|nextest`, `pnpm exec drizzle-kit generate|check`, `docker compose -f docker-compose.db.yml up|down`, read-only git, corpus/rls-verify MCP tools; WebFetch domains: tauri.app, v2.tauri.app, hono.dev, orm.drizzle.team, postgresql.org, react.dev, vite.dev, tailwindcss.com, ui.shadcn.com, learn.microsoft.com, nodejs.org, owasp.org, w3.org, npmjs.com
- **ask**: `pnpm tauri build`, `drizzle-kit migrate`, `docker` (other), `gh release *`, `signtool *`, anything network-mutating
- `defaultMode: acceptEdits`, `disableBypassPermissionsMode: "disable"`, hook wiring with per-hook timeouts, statusline, Stop block cap env.

### MCP servers (tools/mcp/, wired via .mcp.json)
- `corpus-search-server.mjs` — verbatim port incl. the ${...}-placeholder-ignoring CORPUS_INDEX_URL fix; new version-pinned corpus/index.json (Tauri 2 security/config, Drizzle RLS, Hono, Entra OIDC, pgvector, llama.cpp entries). pgvector-backed Stage-2 deferred (same tool contract).
- `rls-verify-server.mjs` — ported: connects via ADMIN_DATABASE_URL, allowlists table/column against information_schema BEFORE building SQL, BEGIN READ ONLY + ROLLBACK, `SET LOCAL ROLE app_api` + `set_config('app.user_sub', A, true)`, requires seeded positive control (B owns ≥1 row) else SKIPPED, returns ISOLATED/LEAK/SKIPPED — never false-green.

## Installer changes (installer/ — mechanics preserved, surface remapped)

- **detect.mjs**: bootstrap = empty/near-empty dir. Retrofit = `pnpm-workspace.yaml` present AND (a tauri.conf.json OR hono dep OR apps/ dir) → monorepo retrofit path. Hard-reject with actionable messages: single-root `src/` layouts, npm/yarn lockfiles ("convert to pnpm first"), and Next.js signatures ("use next-supabase-agent-harness"). v0.1 retrofit merges the ROOT package.json only (same never-clobber semantics: colliding `validate` → project keeps body, harness lands at `harness:validate`); workspace packages are installed only when the path is absent (additive), never merged file-by-file — honest scope, documented.
- **layout.mjs**: RENAMES keeps gitignore/github/gitattributes/editorconfig/mcp.json/env.example + drops nvmrc→ keeps as `.node-version`, drops prettierrc (Biome-only). SEEDED_PREFIXES: `apps/`, `packages/schema/src/`, `packages/importer/`, `packages/eval/`, `docs/adr/`. CONFIG_FILES (seeded-but-hash-tracked): `tools/harness.config.mjs`, `apps/desktop/src-tauri/identity.lock.json`, `size-budgets.json`. OWNED: tools/**, .claude/**, .github/workflows/**, root lint/type configs, packages/config/**. CONFLICTABLE extended with workspace-relative paths: `apps/desktop/vite.config.ts`, `apps/desktop/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json`, `packages/schema/drizzle.config.ts`, plus root biome.jsonc/tsconfig/knip/lefthook/commitlint/vitest/pnpm-workspace/.mcp.json (matching now walks one level into workspaces).
- **placeholders.mjs**: keep the 5 (declaration-order derivation preserved) + `PRODUCT_IDENTIFIER` (default derives `com.<GITHUB_OWNER>.<PROJECT_SLUG>`, feeds tauri.conf.json identifier + identity.lock.json) + `WINDOWS_PUBLISHER` (default = PROJECT_NAME). Entra tenant/client IDs are runtime env (settings.local/env.example), NOT placeholders — keeps the shipped artifact generic and hygiene-clean.
- **manifest/update/doctor/enable**: unchanged mechanics (post-render SHA-256, owned-only upgrades, .harness/pending parking, exit 0/1/2). doctor wiring probes retargeted: harness.config exports non-empty arrays, settings.json wires 5 hooks, tauri.conf.json matches identity.lock.json, drizzle.config resolves, `cargo --version`/`rustup` present (warn), docker reachable (info-only), no unrendered {{tokens}}.
- **plugin channel**: .claude-plugin/{plugin,marketplace}.json point at template/base/.claude/* — dual-channel parity preserved; `--consume` bootstrap path unchanged.

## Template (shipped) CI — template/base/github/workflows/, stored dotless
- **quality-gate.yml**: `static` job (ubuntu): pinned webkit apt deps + rust-cache + `node tools/validate.mjs --min-floor`; `unit` job (vitest projects); `runtime` job (pgvector service container: run-rls for real — replaces `supabase start`); `desktop-windows` job **paths-filtered** to apps/desktop/** + src-tauri/** with timeout-minutes + concurrency cancel-in-progress (consuming projects are private → 2× Windows billing is real; frugality enforced by a workflow-lint rule: no windows job on bare `pull_request` without paths filter).
- **release.yml (shipped)**: tag-triggered DAG — build (windows, `--locked`/`--frozen-lockfile`, SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH) → `node tools/check-signing.mjs` → artifact-set assert → checksums → publish. **Honest signing degradation**: check-signing reads AZURE_CLIENT_ID/TENANT_ID/ENDPOINT/ACCOUNT/PROFILE — ALL present → signCommand was active, run `signtool verify /pa /all` on every .exe + timestamp check + subject == identity.lock.json expectedSigningSubject, FAIL on any miss; NONE present → build unsigned, print a loud UNSIGNED banner, suffix the release title `-unsigned`, and **skip publishing latest.json/updater artifacts** (unsigned builds must never feed an updater); PARTIAL secrets → hard fail (misconfiguration, worst case). Never a silent pass-as-signed.
- Parity set: codeql.yml (javascript-typescript + rust? rust CodeQL still preview → js-ts only, cargo-audit/cargo-deny cover Rust), osv-scan.yml (pnpm-lock.yaml AND Cargo.lock), gitleaks via direct MIT binary (NOT gitleaks-action — license key + Node20 timebombs), actions-lint.yml, zizmor.yml, renovate.json (config:best-practices, pin digests, minimumReleaseAge), CODEOWNERS ({{SECURITY_OWNERS}} on packages/schema, src/auth, capabilities, workflows), PR template (paste real run-rls output; NVDA checklist on release-labelled PRs).

## The reference app: "notes" again — deliberately boring, one vertical slice through every trust boundary

The stack equivalent of the sibling's notes app: a **notes table** (id uuid, owner_sub text, title, body, created_at) that exists to prove the doctrine, not to preview the curriculum product. The 10k-node graph, coverage matrix, xlsx ingestion, and HITL QA are consuming-project work — the harness proves the RAILS they'll run on.

**What ships and what each piece proves:**
- **packages/schema**: `pgTable` + `pgPolicy` ×4 per-op policies keyed on `(SELECT current_setting('app.user_sub', true))` (initPlan), drizzle-zod DTOs, committed generated migration + a roles migration (CREATE ROLE migrator/app_api with NOBYPASSRLS/NOSUPERUSER, grants, `FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY`). Proves: schema-first RLS is TypeScript-visible and gate-checkable (schema-rls + migrations-drift gates pass non-vacuously).
- **apps/server**: Hono + @hono/zod-openapi notes routes; `verifyEntraToken()` (jose, createRemoteJWKSet, iss/aud/tid/alg pinning, clockTolerance 300) behind **AUTH_MODE=entra|stub** — stub mode swaps ONLY the key source (local generated JWKS) and issuer value, so the verification code path is byte-identical and fully testable offline (wrong-aud/iss/alg:none/expired/±skew negative tests run in unit-node with zero network); boot-time fatal if NODE_ENV=production && AUTH_MODE=stub. `withUser(sub, fn)` DAL wrapper (transaction + `set_config(...,true)`); DTO `.strict().parse()` at DAL exit with a canary-extra-column test; pino logger with redaction test; `/api/meta` + version-skew middleware with the structural route-coverage vitest (every route except meta/healthz sits behind it). Committed openapi.json. Proves: Entra doctrine without an Azure tenant; the DAL is the authz boundary; contracts can't drift.
- **apps/desktop**: Vite + React 19 + TS max-strict + Tailwind v4 `@theme` OKLCH tokens + one shadcn component; notes list screen using the typed `hc<AppType>` client via TanStack Query; keyboard-shortcut registry module (the WCAG 2.1.4-safe typed registry) with one wired shortcut; `src/ipc/` as the sole `invoke()` site calling one `#[tauri::command]` (`get_app_info` with a validated-path example command + negative Rust tests). **src-tauri/**: tauri.conf.json (targets ["nsis"], perMachine, offlineInstaller, strict CSP, freezePrototype, no remote), ONE capability file, identity.lock.json, rust-toolchain.toml, `[workspace.lints]` (unsafe_code=forbid, unwrap/expect/panic deny). Proves: the tauri-policy gate has a real green subject; cargo check + debug NSIS bundle + silent-install smoke all pass in CI.
- **Desktop auth**: an `AuthProvider` interface with `StubAuthProvider` (dev token from local stub) wired; the full PKCE-via-system-browser + loopback flow is a documented skeleton in the skill reference, NOT shipped code (v0.1 cut — no way to make it bootstrap-green without a tenant; the server-side verification, which is where the security lives, IS fully proven).
- **packages/importer**: CSV importer skeleton (zero heavy deps — xlsx is consuming-project territory), Result-typed errors, one pinned fixture, one fast-check round-trip property test. Proves: the property-test lane and parse-don't-validate pattern exist and run.
- **packages/eval**: `InferenceProvider`/`EmbeddingProvider` ports; `ReplayProvider` over one committed recording; `LlamaCppProvider` that throws under EVAL_MODE=replay; undici MockAgent disableNetConnect in test setup (a live call is a test failure, structurally); one eval case + committed baseline metrics + `check-eval-baseline.mjs`; prompts/ with content-addressed frontmatter + version-lint. Proves: hermetic replay evals with a swappable provider, no GPU anywhere in the default path.
- **tests/rls/** + docker-compose.db.yml: catalog SQL assertions + behavioral isolation with seeded positive controls against `pgvector/pgvector:pg16` (image proves the pgvector pin story even though notes has no vector column; a `vector(8)` demo column + dimension contract test rides on the notes table so the extension is exercised).

**Ballooning guard**: one table, one route family, one screen, one command, one fixture each. Anything that needs a second table to demonstrate belongs in the skill references or a module.

risks: Cold `cargo check` at first validate (fresh install or evicted CI cache) takes minutes and will read as 'the gate is broken' — mitigated by the content-hash stamp, the nightly cache-warmer schedule, and a loud CLAUDE.md/doctor note, but the first-turn experience on a new machine is unavoidably slow; do not let anyone 'fix' it by dropping rust-check from the FLOOR.

Ubuntu Tauri compilation depends on a pinned apt package list (libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev etc.) that drifts with runner images — every linux job that runs validate must carry the same pinned list, and an image update can break bootstrap-green overnight; the nightly selftest schedule exists to catch this before a user does.

drizzle-kit is mid-1.0.0-rc churn: an unpinned bump can change migration file layout and silently break the migrations-drift gate and the append-only migration guard — drizzle-kit must be EXACT-pinned in the catalog and Renovate-cooldowned, and the drift gate's failure message must say 'regenerate, don't hand-edit'.

The contracts and schema-rls gates import TypeScript app code via tsx at gate time — a syntax error in apps/server makes them fail with confusing stack traces before `types` would have caught it cleanly; gate ordering (types before contracts) is load-bearing and the scripts need try/catch wrappers that say 'fix types first'.

Shipped quality-gate runtime job assumes Docker/service containers exist — consuming on-prem/self-hosted runners without Docker will hard-fail the fail-closed RLS gate; docs must cover the self-hosted-runner path (native Postgres + DATABASE_URL) or projects will delete the job, which is the worst outcome.

Signing degradation can be socially misread: an '-unsigned' release still produces an installable .exe, and a team may ship it internally for months — the release notes banner, DEPLOYMENT.md, and the updater-manifest refusal are the guardrails, but nothing mechanical stops sideloading an unsigned build.

Windows CI flakiness (corepack/pnpm shims, PowerShell quoting, NSIS silent-install races, WebView2 offlineInstaller CDN fetch during bundling) will be the #1 source of red selftest runs — budget real hardening time for bootstrap-windows and tauri-build-smoke, and keep harden-runner in audit mode on Windows (block is unsupported there; claiming it would silently downgrade).

Other perspectives will likely propose defaults that break bootstrap-green or Stop-hook latency: isolation pattern by default (fiddly dist-isolation wiring), tauri-specta bindings drift gate (codegen at bootstrap), Vitest Browser Mode/WDIO/mutation in STOP_HOOK_STEPS (20+ minute turns get gamed), pgTAP in the default runtime image, mandatory Entra tenant config at init, and REUSE per-file SPDX headers (mass churn) — each is designed here as a named module instead; hold that line.

knip/depcruise false positives on Tauri- and Vite-shaped entrypoints (commands invoked from Rust by string, GBNF/fixture assets, vite config plugins) will tempt agents toward blanket ignores — knip.json is write-guard-protected and `knip --fix` is bash-guard-denied, but expect tuning churn in the first weeks.

TypeScript 6/7 transition timing: the design pins TS 6.0.x with typed-lint (typescript-eslint 8.6x); if implementation slips past typescript-eslint's TS7 support announcement the pin choice should be revisited at build time, not inherited blindly from this plan.
summary:
Build BhodiSea/tauri-postgres-agent-harness as a mechanical port of the sibling harness's proven kernel (three-tier hook exit-code contract, single-source gate config consumed by Stop hook + pnpm validate + CI with a hardcoded --min-floor, manifest-hashed installer with merge-never-clobber retrofit, dual npx/plugin channels, selftest-as-release-acceptance) onto a pnpm monorepo stack — with every stack-specific surface re-derived from the 14 research reports and every default gate held to two hard bars: it must pass on a fresh install with zero external accounts (no Azure tenant, no signing cert, no GPU, no Docker required locally), and the full Stop-hook chain must stay under ~90 seconds warm. The delivery thesis: cheap deterministic JSON/SQL/grep gates (tauri-policy, schema-rls, migrations-drift, contracts, check-rust with a content-hash stamp) encode the bulk of the research's ~140 recommendations at near-zero runtime cost, while everything heavy or environment-dependent (WDIO E2E, mutation, SBOM/NOTICES, Sentry, pgBackRest, isolation pattern, specta bindings, live evals, macOS) becomes one of 16 named opt-in modules so v0.1 is shippable without abandoning the doctrine.

The repo's own CI is the product proof: bootstrap-green on BOTH ubuntu (with the pinned webkit apt list Tauri needs) and windows-latest on every PR, a real `pnpm tauri build --debug` → silent-install → uninstall smoke lane on windows-latest (paths-filtered on PRs, nightly to keep the Rust cache alive past GitHub's 7-day eviction), a pgvector/pg16 runtime job where the RLS suite provably RUNS rather than self-skips, a retrofit non-clobber job, hook exit-code contract tests, and a both-directions violation canary. Signing degrades honestly: unsigned builds are loudly labeled, never verify-skipped-silently, and never feed the updater manifest. Critical implementation files (all under the new repo, mechanics cloned from /Users/thomasnicklin/Desktop/next-supabase-agent-harness): installer/lib/layout.mjs + detect.mjs (monorepo remap), template/base/tools/harness.config.mjs + tools/validate.mjs (gate chain + FLOOR), template/base/.claude/hooks/pretool-write-guard.mjs (PROTECTED list + content checks), template/base/tools/check-tauri-policy.mjs (the highest-leverage new gate), and .github/workflows/selftest.yml (the acceptance matrix).
